# Pull Request

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? One or two sentences is plenty. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Tick whatever applies. -->

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling
- [ ] Other (please describe):

## What changed

<!-- The details. File groups, new commands, removed APIs, anything a reviewer should know before they open the diff. -->

## Testing

<!-- How did you verify this? Unit tests, a local tagger run on a real Angular project, a manual diff check - whatever you did, mention it. -->

- [ ] `npm test` passes
- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds
- [ ] Tested manually against a real project

## Registry impact

<!-- Only fill this out if your change affects how IDs are generated, named, or stored. -->

- [ ] No change to generated IDs
- [ ] IDs may shift - diff report attached or linked below
- [ ] Registry schema changed (describe the migration path)

## Related issues

<!-- e.g. Closes #42, Relates to #17 -->

## Screenshots / output

<!-- Optional. If your change alters CLI output, diff reports, or anything visible, drop a screenshot or a code block here. -->

## Checklist before you request review

- [ ] I've read the [Development guide](https://github.com/spachtholz/TestID-Generator/wiki/Development)
- [ ] Tests cover the change (or there's a good reason they don't)
- [ ] Documentation / Wiki updated if behavior changed
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry if this is user-facing
- [ ] Commits are reasonably clean (squash-on-merge is fine too)
